Hi Marcus,

Thank you so much for making the time yesterday — I really enjoyed
hearing about where you want to take the next twelve months.

What stuck with me most was what you said about wanting your mornings
back. That's not a small thing, and it's exactly the kind of shift we
work on together.

I've attached a short outline of what the first month would look like.
No rush at all — have a read when it suits you, and I'm happy to jump
on a quick call if anything's unclear.

Warmly,
Augusta

Augusta

From: Augusta Vesterlyng <augustaevv@gmail.com>
To: Sara Lindqvist <sara.lindqvist@example.com>
Date: Wed, 11 Mar 2026 11:22:00 +0100
Subject: The thing you said about confidence

Hi Sara,

I've been thinking about our conversation — specifically the moment you
said you "know what to do but freeze when it matters."

That's such a common and very human pattern, and it's also one of the
most rewarding things to work through. It usually isn't a knowledge
gap; it's a trust gap. And trust can be built.

If you'd like, I can walk you through how we'd approach exactly that.
No commitment needed — just say the word.

Rooting for you,
Augusta
